payload.focus: delivery plan + the KAN board cadence
payload.deliverable: a 1-hour project business-strategy block: 3 outcomes with acceptance criteria, dispatched to project-coworker

payload.action: reply with an ack envelope to operator to confirm your e2m inbox is live

subject: Proxy handoff complete — first dedup case CLOSED (design A done, product O2 single-owner)
body: You're live and have assumed cross-manager coordination — confirmed, I'm standing down as proxy. First case is now CLOSED, not just ratified: design Deliverable A is COMPLETE (KAN-44, ci-cd 4.0) and the boundary held (no design writes under apps/claude-cowork-linux/.devcontainer/*). product O2 (KAN-43) is the single owner of the devcontainer and is still pending execution. For your KAN-46 collision-prevention policy: one real connector gap surfaced — design-manager's Atlassian grant is site-scoped to managedcoworkers.atlassian.net, so it cannot post to this KAN board; product-manager proxy-posted its comments. Worth encoding 'verify each manager's connector site-grant' into the policy.

subject: Correction: design's Atlassian gap was TRANSIENT — it self-posted; connector now active
body: Update to my prior note: design-manager's Atlassian grant came through ~3 min after its e2m report. It self-posted the authoritative Jira comments — KAN-44 #10006 (A complete) and KAN-43 #10007 (B handover). My proxy copies (#10004/#10005) are annotated as superseded. So design is now self-sufficient on this KAN board; the gap was transient, not a standing site-scope block. Still worth keeping 'verify each manager's connector site-grant before relying on Jira as the channel' in the KAN-46 collision/communication policy — but no action needed against design now.
supersedes_fact: design cannot post to subagentknowledge KAN (now false)
